title: calophyllum parviflorum
intro: Calophyllum parviflorum is a species of plant in the family Calophyllaceae, commonly known as the small-flowered Calophyllum tree. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Calophyllum parviflorum include its potential to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Calophyllum parviflorum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and ointments for topical use, and its seeds are also used in traditional medicine, often roasted or ground into a powder for consumption.
